grants.name: Victorian Sporting Club Grants Program (Round 2)
grants.notes: 5 funding categories. Must be incorporated legal entity delivering sport/active recreation. Strong fit for SuperNova equipment, women's program launch (Access & Events category), or volunteer development. Apply via DJSIR Grants Portal.

grants.name: ASF Active Kids Grant 2026
grants.notes: Uniforms and equipment for volunteer-run clubs with members aged 20 or under. Awarded as in-kind equipment/uniforms, not cash. Uniform orders minimum $2,200 GST inclusive. Perfect for SuperNova U18 division. Average grant value $3,500–$4,500 in previous rounds.

grants.name: Wyndham Medium Grants – Round 2
grants.notes: Strongest council opportunity — both orgs are Wyndham-based. Requires 2:1 co-contribution ratio (minimum $7K co-contribution for $14K grant). In-kind volunteer hours valued at $45/hour count toward co-contribution. Ideal for Holi event or Women's Badminton launch. Incorporated NFP required.

grants.name: Hobsons Bay Make it Happen Grants
grants.notes: Largest single council grant available. Total program pool $495,000. Events & Festivals category ideal for TVS multicultural festivals. Major Events must meet 3+ criteria including tourism benefits and profile enhancement. All events must operate within Hobsons Bay — need to confirm presence or partnerships. Separate event permit required.

grants.name: Melton Community Grants 2025/26
grants.notes: Rolling assessment every two months (next round closes 30 Apr). Up to $10,000 per financial year across multiple projects. Must benefit Melton residents and be completed within 12 months. Mandatory consultation with Council Project Officer before applying. 2 quotes required for items over $1,000. Public liability insurance $20M minimum.

grants.name: Wyndham Small Grants
grants.notes: Rolling applications until 31 May 2026. Quick wins for equipment or small program costs. Up to $500 for individuals and unincorporated groups, up to $2,000 for incorporated NFPs. Both organisations are Wyndham-based — easy eligibility.

grants.name: Wyndham Community Pathways Scholarships
grants.notes: Individual scholarships for Wyndham residents pursuing achievements in sport, recreation, arts, or academics. Could support SuperNova's high-performing U18 players attending state or national badminton competitions.

grants.name: Local Sporting Champions (Round 4)
grants.notes: For individual coaches, officials and competitors aged 12–18 attending state, national or international championships. Base grant $500–$750 plus $200 bonus for international or 2,000km+ travel, plus $50 for rural electorates. Could support SuperNova U18 players attending badminton championships.

grants.name: Federal Volunteer Grants 2025–26
grants.notes: Expressions of Interest submitted through local Federal MP — contact their electorate office ASAP. Each electorate has $66,665 to allocate. Must have minimum 40% volunteer workforce. Both organisations are heavily volunteer-driven — strong natural alignment. Two-stage process: EOI to MP first, then invited to full application in May 2026.

grants.name: City of Melbourne Connected Neighbourhoods
grants.notes: Monthly assessment rounds. For community-led initiatives promoting connection, inclusion and participation at neighbourhood/suburb level. Only relevant if TVS delivers neighbourhood-level programs within City of Melbourne municipal boundaries. NFP or auspiced required.

grants.name: City of Melbourne – Town Halls Scheme (CUTHS)
categories: Free venue use for NFP events
grants.notes: In-kind support for NFP organisations to use Melbourne Town Halls at no cost. Could be useful if TVS holds a community event within City of Melbourne municipality. NFP status required.

closedGrants.name: Monash Community Grants 2025/26
closedGrants.reason: Closed March 2025. No current rounds on SmartyGrants. Watch for 2026/27 round opening approximately January 2027.

closedGrants.name: City of Melbourne Community Inclusion Grants 2026-27
closedGrants.reason: Closed 9 February 2026. Projects run July 2026–June 2027. Next round likely opens late 2026.

closedGrants.name: City of Melbourne Community Events Grants 2026-27
closedGrants.reason: Closed 26 January 2026. Events July 2026–June 2027. Next round likely opens late 2026.

closedGrants.name: Change Our Game Community Activation Grants
closedGrants.reason: Closed for 2024-25. Up to $10K for women in sport initiatives. Watch for 2025-26 round at changeourgame.vic.gov.au — excellent fit for SuperNova Women's program.

closedGrants.name: VicHealth Growing Healthy Communities (Round 2)
closedGrants.reason: Closed. Tier 1: $10K–$50K, Tier 2: $50K–$150K. Funded projects delivered March 2026–March 2028.

closedGrants.name: Multicultural Festivals & Events Program 2024-25
closedGrants.reason: Rounds 1 and 2 both closed. Up to $50K for metropolitan events. Watch for 2025-26 program opening — ideal for TVS Holi/Diwali events.

closedGrants.name: Wyndham Arts Grants 2026
closedGrants.reason: Closed 14 January 2026. Creative Development up to $3K, Presentation up to $5K.

upcomingGrants.name: City of Melbourne Annual Arts Grants 2027
upcomingGrants.note: For one-off arts projects presented within City of Melbourne municipality. Open to individuals, groups and small-to-medium arts organisations. Art residencies also available (6 or 12 months, up to $5K).

upcomingGrants.name: Change Our Game Community Activation Grants 2025-26
upcomingGrants.note: Perfect for SuperNova Women's Badminton program. Three streams: Participation (new women's programs), Education (gender equity training), Community Leaders (professional development for women volunteers). Must be incorporated NFP delivering sport.

upcomingGrants.name: Multicultural Festivals & Events Program 2025-26 (new round)
upcomingGrants.note: Strongest potential fit for TVS multicultural events (Holi, Diwali, Lunar New Year). Metro and regional streams available. Contact multicultural-festivals.events@dpc.vic.gov.au for announcements.

upcomingGrants.name: VicHealth Active Club Grants (next round)
upcomingGrants.note: Historically supports women and girls' participation and new social/modified sport programs. Strong fit for SuperNova women's drop-in socials and pennant league launch.
